summary: The suite covers all five major requirement areas: all three retention policies (max-age, keep-latest, max-total-size) are exercised individually and in combination, the deletion plan format and summary totals are verified, dry-run mode is checked, and error paths (missing file, malformed TSV, unknown flag, missing --input) are all represented. Stdin input is tested as a bonus. Rigor is the weak spot: boundary conditions are mostly absent — there is no test for an artifact aged exactly N days (the boundary between KEEP and DELETE), no test for numeric policy args receiving non-integer strings (the implementation validates this but no test drives it), no test for an already-under-cap max-total-size (zero deletions expected), no empty-input test, and no tie-breaking verification when two artifacts share the same date within a workflow. The combined-policy test only asserts aggregate counts rather than which specific artifacts are retained or deleted, reducing its regression value. Design is solid: setup/teardown isolates each test in a fresh tmpdir, fixtures are descriptively named, bats 'run' is used correctly throughout, and grouping by concern with comment banners makes the file easy to navigate. One minor smell: redefining TMPDIR in setup() shadows the system env var, which can cause unexpected behaviour on some platforms.
